# PICO-8 cart context (generated by `picopilot init`)

You are editing a PICO-8 cart with picopilot. This file is your always-loaded
reference: the constraints, the loop, the palette, the memory map (+ the overlap
warning), the most-used API, and how to stay under the token budget. It is
curated from PICO-8's manual for the things models get wrong without a reminder.

## The `#include` discipline (how this cart is laid out)

- `main.p8` is the cart. Its `__lua__` section is a single line: `#include main.lua`.
- `main.lua` is the plain Lua you EDIT. Never hand-write `main.p8`'s binary
  sections (`__gfx__`, `__map__`, `__sfx__`, `__music__`), use picopilot's
  `gfx` / audio commands, which edit them safely and round-trippably.

## Hard specs (the constraints that shape everything)

- Display: **128x128**, a **fixed 16-colour palette** (indices 0..15).
- Code: **Lua**, max **8192 tokens** (the #1 thing to watch). Run `picopilot tokens`.
- Sprites: 256 8x8 sprites in a base bank (0..127) plus a shared bank (128..255).
- Map: 128x32 tiles + a shared half that ALIASES sprites 128..255 (see below).
- Sound: 4 channels, 64 SFX slots; music is patterns of up to 4 SFX references.
- Numbers: 16.16 fixed point, range `-32768.0 .. 32767.99`.

## The execution loop (every cart)

- `_init()`: runs once at startup.
- `_update()`: runs every update tick at 30fps (`_update60()` for 60fps).
- `_draw()`: runs once per visible frame.

A cart with none of these is inert. Keep them defined.

## The fixed 16-colour palette (index â†’ RGB)

In a char grid, `.` = transparent and `0-F` = these indices.

| idx | hex | name        | R   | G   | B   |
|-----|-----|-------------|-----|-----|-----|
| 0   | 0   | black       | 0   | 0   | 0   |
| 1   | 1   | dark-blue   | 29  | 43  | 83  |
| 2   | 2   | dark-purple | 126 | 37  | 83  |
| 3   | 3   | dark-green  | 0   | 135 | 81  |
| 4   | 4   | brown       | 171 | 82  | 54  |
| 5   | 5   | dark-grey   | 95  | 87  | 79  |
| 6   | 6   | light-grey  | 194 | 195 | 199 |
| 7   | 7   | white       | 255 | 241 | 232 |
| 8   | 8   | red         | 255 | 0   | 77  |
| 9   | 9   | orange      | 255 | 163 | 0   |
| 10  | a   | yellow      | 255 | 236 | 39  |
| 11  | b   | green       | 0   | 228 | 54  |
| 12  | c   | blue        | 41  | 173 | 255 |
| 13  | d   | lavender    | 131 | 118 | 156 |
| 14  | e   | pink        | 255 | 119 | 168 |
| 15  | f   | peach       | 255 | 204 | 170 |

## Memory map + the gfx/map overlap warning

```
0x0000  gfx (spritesheet, sprites 0..127)
0x1000  shared gfx / map2   <-- sprites 128..255 ALIAS the bottom half of the map
0x2000  map
0x3000  gfx flags
0x3100  music
0x3200  sfx
0x4300  user data
0x5e00  persistent cart data (256 bytes)
0x5f00  draw state
0x5f40  hardware state
0x5f80  gpio pins
0x6000  screen (8k)
```

**Overlap warning:** sprites 128..255 (`0x1000`-`0x1fff`) occupy the SAME memory
as `__map__` rows 32..63. Writing a sprite in that range clobbers the map tiles
there. `picopilot gfx set` smart-refuses such a write when real map tiles are at
risk and nothing authorised the loss (`--allow-map-overlap` or `allowMapOverlap`
in `picopilot.json`), so you never silently destroy map data you cannot see.

## Most-used API (grouped)

- **Graphics:** `cls([col])`, `spr(n,x,y,[w,h],[fx],[fy])`, `sspr(...)`,
  `map(cx,cy,sx,sy,cw,ch,[layer])`, `pset/pget`, `sget/sset`,
  `print(str,[x,y,[col]])`, `rect/rectfill`, `circ/circfill`, `oval/ovalfill`,
  `line`, `pal(c0,c1,[p])`, `palt(col,t)`, `camera([x,y])`, `clip([x,y,w,h])`,
  `color(col)`, `fillp(mask)`, `tline(...)`, `flip()`.
- **Map / flags:** `mget(x,y)`, `mset(x,y,v)`, `fget(n,[f])`, `fset(n,[f],v)`.
- **Input:** `btn([i,[p]])`, `btnp([i,[p]])` (buttons 0..5 = left,right,up,down,O/Z,X).
- **Audio:** `sfx(n,[channel,[offset]])`, `music([n,[fade,[mask]]])`.
- **Math:** `abs`, `flr` (`-flr(-x)` = ceil), `min`, `max`, `mid`, `rnd(x)`,
  `srand(x)`, `sgn`, `sqrt`, `sin(x)`/`cos(x)` (range 0..1, sin inverted),
  `atan2(x,y)`.
- **Tables:** `add`, `del`, `deli`, `count`, `all(t)`, `foreach(t,f)`, `pairs`, `ipairs`.
- **Memory:** `peek`, `poke`, `memcpy`, `memset`, `cstore`, `reload`.
- **Cart data:** `cartdata(id)`, `dget(i)`, `dset(i,v)`.
- **Debug:** `printh(str)` (to host stdout, `picopilot run` captures it), `stat(n)`.

## Token discipline (stay under 8192)

Models blow the budget by writing verbose Lua. Prefer PICO-8 shorthands:

- `?` for `print`.
- inline if: `if(c) x=1`.
- compound assignment: `+=`, `-=`, `*=`, `/=`.
- `\` for integer divide.
- reuse locals; avoid needless globals.

Run `picopilot tokens` each iteration to see the count and where it goes; when
over budget, `picopilot minify` (safe-only) reclaims tokens without changing
behaviour.
